name: Armenian Pumpkin Stew
recipeIngredient: 2 carrots, peeled and cut into pieces
1 celery root, peeled and cut into pieces
4 large red ripe tomatoes, peeled, cored and sliced
1 acorn squash, peeled and cut into cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the coriander, cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, and clove in a spice mill or coffee grinder. Grind until smooth. Set aside.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large, heavy-bottom saucepan over medium heat. Add the lamb in one layer. Sprinkle with the spice mixture. Cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 3-5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pan and set aside.
3. Add the onion and garlic to the pan. Saute, stirring frequently, until translucent, about 5 minutes.
4. Add the carrots, celery root, tomatoes, and acorn squash. Add the broth. Return the lamb to the pan. Partly cover and gently simmer until the lamb is tender, about 1.5 to 2 hours. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Place the pumpkin on a baking sheet. Brush the outside with the remaining oil. Bake until tender, about 45 to 60 minutes.
6. Cook the rice according to package directions and set aside.
7. To assemble, place the pumpkin in a serving dish. Fill with the lamb stew. Divide the rice among 4 warmed bowls. Ladle the stew from the pumpkin over the rice. Garnish with coriander and parsley. Serve immediately.
Yield: 4-6 servings
